action.reasoning: Observation: Ensemble (ConvNeXt+CLIP) got 0.7852, slightly worse than ConvNeXt alone 0.7879. CLIP underperforms in this setup (different data distribution sensitivity). Analysis: Need to either retrain CLIP better (longer training, lower LR) or try a different angle. Best result so far is single ConvNeXt-Tiny with combined train+val + mixup + LS + cosine for 4 epochs. To push higher: (1) ensemble of multiple ConvNeXt runs with different seeds, (2) use cleanlab to identify clean samples and re-train, (3) higher resolution. Let me try: train two more ConvNeXt-Tiny models with different seeds and ensemble all three. Each run ~3.5 min. Or, try using cleanlab confident learning to filter noise. With 4.65h remaining and 18 iterations, I can afford a more careful approach. Decision: Switch strategy - train CLIP ViT-B/16 better (5 epochs, longer warmup, more careful schedule). If still bad, drop it. Actually simpler: train another ConvNeXt-Tiny with different seed and ensemble two ConvNeXts.
